title: Self-harm history documentation prompts
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports collaborative psychosocial assessment after self-harm for self-harm history documentation; no decision is generated.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

red_flags.text: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.
red_flags.rationale: Supports initial assessment and care after self-harm for self-harm history documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.12–1.7.20 — emergency-department initial assessment and care

treatment_and_management.text: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports safeguarding and qualified discharge context for self-harm history documentation; no decision is generated.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Method, timing, frequency, intent, lethality, triggers, medical consequences, current thoughts, means, protective factors, mental state, formulation and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for self-harm history documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
